title: A $3.2 Trillion Deal-Making Frenzy Is Spurred by the A.I. Economy
summary: This year’s boom includes the most spent on global deal-making in a six-month period in a decade. But questions persist about whether it can continue.

title: Grocery Stores Lower Prices as Consumers Pare Spending
summary: While shoppers may get better deals on some items, it’s unlikely their overall grocery bill will fall.

title: Erika Kirk Requests All Evidence in Husband’s Killing to Be Shown Publicly
summary: A lawyer for Charlie Kirk’s family said disclosure would help quell conspiracy theories, but the judge ruled that not all evidence would be shown in the courtroom or on a livestream.

title: Platner’s Exit Sets Off Scramble for New Senate Candidate in Maine
summary: The state Democratic Party has said it will pick a replacement through a nominating convention before a July 27 deadline. Candidates are already lining up.

title: Inside the Implosion of Graham Platner’s Maine Senate Campaign: ‘A Slow-Rolling Disaster’
summary: Graham Platner’s bid for the Senate inspired progressive Democrats. But the campaign, which he suspended Wednesday, was messy, disorganized and ultimately doomed by a steady drip of scandal.

title: Maine Democrats Hope to Replace Platner as Senate Nominee. Has That Ever Worked?
summary: Graham Platner is one of just a handful of candidates in recent decades who have withdrawn from a Senate race after securing a major party nomination.

title: Tanker Attacks Risk Overplaying Iran’s Hand, Analysts Say
summary: Iran’s history shows it is willing to dig in, even when it may suffer big losses.

title: Too Many Books?
summary: Mendel Uminer faced a crisis when his landlord objected to the 10,000 volumes in his New York studio apartment.

title: Ukrainians Cautiously Welcome Trump’s Announcement About Patriot Missile Systems
summary: President Trump said the United States would license Ukraine to produce Patriots, which can intercept ballistic missiles. But it could be months or years before those are ready.

title: For Ukraine, Other Patriot Makers Are a Cautionary Tale
summary: Two American allies, Germany and Japan, already have permission to build the American interceptors, a license that President Trump says he will also grant to Kyiv.

title: Trump’s NATO Summit, and Everyone Else’s
summary: While President Trump insulted allies and demanded loyalty, the military alliance moved quietly closer to accepting more European responsibility for defense.

title: Gaza Aid Worker on His Way to Watch World Cup Killed by Israeli Strike
summary: The Israeli military said it had struck a Hamas militant but was aware of reports that “uninvolved civilians” were harmed.

title: This Summer, Midtown Manhattan Is Taking Center Stage
summary: Midtown, with its high-rise office buildings and tourist hotels, isn’t hip, but it has become the site of a series of dramatic spectacles this year.

title: China, Russia and Others Seek to Inflame Debate Over A.I. Data Centers
summary: State actors in China, Russia and Iran have sought to exploit the U.S. public debate over the effects of the technology.

title: Big Tech Is Now Targeting Native American Land for Massive Data Centers
summary: The data center boom has roiled communities across the country, but on Native land, a Big Tech push for quick approvals has pitted the need for development against a history of exploitation.

title: Wally Funk, Who Set an Age Record for Space Travel, Dies at 87
summary: As a young woman in the 1960s, she wasn’t allowed to become a NASA astronaut. She finally realized her long-held dream of flying in space as an octogenarian.

title: Review: ‘Little House on the Prairie’ Adds On an Expansion
summary: The classic story (and recent culture-war object) remains wholesome but makes room for people who called the prairie home before the Ingalls family.

title: A New ‘Little House’ Broadens and Updates the Prairie
summary: A Netflix adaptation of the beloved tale empowers Ma and adds new Black and Native characters to challenge prejudices. The creators know criticism is coming.

title: How ‘Little House’ Welcomes Native Neighbors
summary: The real Ingalls family built their “little house on the prairie” on Osage land and evinced racist attitudes. A new TV adaptation tries to reckon with this while preserving the story’s heart.

title: ‘There’s No Graham Platner Without Donald Trump’: 3 Writers on the Fiasco in Maine
summary: How much have the rules of politics changed?

title: ‘ I’m Worried About Weimar America’
summary: “It may not be Hitler 2.0. It may not be Stalin 2.0. It might be something all American, but it’s not going to be what we’re used to,” the author Rod Dreher argues.

title: Bonnie Tyler, Who Sang ‘Total Eclipse of the Heart,’ Dies at 75
summary: With a voice both weathered and operatic, she minted 1980s pop anthems like “Total Eclipse” and “Holding Out for a Hero.”
